evals.name: multiple-fails
evals.prompt: check my paper for AEI compliance. Main file is paper.tex, bib is refs.bib.
evals.expected_output: Report should flag: wrong document class (elsarticle not cas-dc/cas-sc); missing affiliations with country; missing corresponding author email; abstract >250 words AND contains a citation; missing Highlights; missing Acknowledgements, CRediT, competing interest, data availability; author-year bib style (plainnat); all bib entries missing DOI; journals not abbreviated (e.g., 'Journal of Machine Learning Research'); unused bib entry 'unused2019'.

evals.name: partial-compliance
evals.prompt: check my paper for AEI compliance
evals.expected_output: Report should flag: abstract contains a \citep{} citation; highlight 3 exceeds 85 characters; missing Data availability section; missing Declaration of generative AI use; figure fig:ablation is defined but never referenced; 2 bib entries missing DOI (doe2021, brown2023); 2 journals not abbreviated ('Advanced Engineering Informatics', 'Journal of Manufacturing Systems'); mixed American/British spelling (analyse/behaviour/optimises vs. color). Should PASS: document class cas-dc, title page, keywords count, Acknowledgements, CRediT, competing interest.

evals.name: near-compliant
evals.prompt: check my paper for AEI compliance please
evals.expected_output: Report should mostly PASS. Only meaningful issue: bib entry jones2022 has journal spelled out as 'Journal of Manufacturing Systems' (should be 'J. Manuf. Syst.'). All other checks should pass — this tests the skill's ability to avoid false positives on a near-compliant paper.
